profile.education: University of Massachusetts Boston (BA); University of Maine School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney; served in Maine House of Representatives and as Maine Attorney General (twice); became Maine's first female governor and 75th governor in 2019.
Former staffer for Senator William Cohen and executive director of the Center for Family Business at Husson University. First elected to Senate in 1996. Known as a centrist Republican.

profile.education: Dartmouth College (BA), University of Virginia School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Independent who caucuses with Democrats. Former Governor of Maine (1995-2003). Energy company founder. Attorney and former aide to Senator William Hathaway. TV host.
Former staffer for Senator William Cohen and executive director of the Center for Family Business at Husson University. First elected to Senate in 1996. Known as a centrist Republican.
